heading: Regulation of services, facilities, and equipment
label: 544 Regulation of services, facilities, and equipment

children.heading: Regulation by franchising authority
children.label: a Regulation by franchising authority
children.text: Any franchising authority may not regulate the services, facilities, and equipment provided by a cable operator except to the extent consistent with this subchapter.

children.heading: Requests for proposals; establishment and enforcement of requirements
children.label: b Requests for proposals; establishment and enforcement of requirements

children.text: in its request for proposals for a franchise (including requests for renewal proposals, subject to ), may establish requirements for facilities and equipment, but may not, except as provided in subsection (h), establish requirements for video programming or other information services; andsection 546 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: for facilities and equipment; and

children.label: B
children.text: for broad categories of video programming or other services.

children.heading: Enforcement authority respecting franchises effective under prior law
children.label: c Enforcement authority respecting franchises effective under prior law
children.text: In the case of any franchise in effect on the effective date of this subchapter, the franchising authority may, subject to , enforce requirements contained within the franchise for the provision of services, facilities, and equipment, whether or not related to the establishment or operation of a cable system.section 545 of this title

children.heading: Cable service unprotected by Constitution; blockage of premium channel upon request
children.label: d Cable service unprotected by Constitution; blockage of premium channel upon request

children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed as prohibiting a franchising authority and a cable operator from specifying, in a franchise or renewal thereof, that certain cable services shall not be provided or shall be provided subject to conditions, if such cable services are obscene or are otherwise unprotected by the Constitution of the United States.

In order to restrict the viewing of of of programming which is obscene or indecent, upon the request of a subscriber, a cable operator shall provide (by sale or lease) a device by which the subscriber can prohibit viewing of a particular cable service during periods selected by that subscriber.1So in original.1

children.label: i
children.text: notify all cable subscribers that the cable operator plans to provide a premium channel without charge;

children.label: ii
children.text: notify all cable subscribers when the cable operator plans to offer a premium channel without charge;

children.label: iii
children.text: notify all cable subscribers that they have a right to request that the channel carrying the premium channel be blocked; and

children.label: iv
children.text: block the channel carrying the premium channel upon the request of a subscriber.

children.label: B
children.text: For the purpose of this section, the term “premium channel” shall mean any pay service offered on a per channel or per program basis, which offers movies rated by the Motion Picture Association of America as X, NC–17, or R.

children.heading: Technical standards
children.label: e Technical standards
children.text: Within one year after , the Commission shall prescribe regulations which establish minimum technical standards relating to cable systems’ technical operation and signal quality. The Commission shall update such standards periodically to reflect improvements in technology. No State or franchising authority may prohibit, condition, or restrict a cable system’s use of any type of subscriber equipment or any transmission technology.October 5, 1992

children.heading: Limitation on regulatory powers of Federal agencies, States, or franchising authorities; exceptions
children.label: f Limitation on regulatory powers of Federal agencies, States, or franchising authorities; exceptions

children.text: Any Federal agency, State, or franchising authority may not impose requirements regarding the provision or content of cable services, except as expressly provided in this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: any rule, regulation, or order issued under any Federal law, as such rule, regulation, or order (i) was in effect on , or (ii) may be amended after such date if the rule, regulation, or order as amended is not inconsistent with the express provisions of this subchapter; andSeptember 21, 1983

children.label: B
children.text: any rule, regulation, or order under title 17.

children.heading: Access to emergency information
children.label: g Access to emergency information
children.text: Notwithstanding any such rule, regulation, or order, each cable operator shall comply with such standards as the Commission shall prescribe to ensure that viewers of video programming on cable systems are afforded the same emergency information as is afforded by the emergency broadcasting system pursuant to Commission regulations in subpart G of part 73, title 47, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Notice of changes in and comments on services
children.label: h Notice of changes in and comments on services

children.text: Provide 30 days’ advance written notice of any change in channel assignment or in the video programming service provided over any such channel.
Inform subscribers, via written notice, that comments on programming and channel position changes are being recorded by a designated office of the franchising authority.

children.heading: Disposition of cable upon termination of service
children.label: i Disposition of cable upon termination of service
children.text: Within 120 days after , the Commission shall prescribe rules concerning the disposition, after a subscriber to a cable system terminates service, of any cable installed by the cable operator within the premises of such subscriber.October 5, 1992
